code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test08-MAR-2020Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and glucosuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Markedly low HbA1c with concurrent glucosuria and ketonuria may indicate recent onset or poorly controlled diabetes or another metabolic disturbance. Recommend further nephrological and metabolic evaluation.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
